body.title: Load matching persistent-relationship facia as primary TWM attractor on turn entry
body.result: 72978a10 — Frame marker, not content flood. Refined pre-sprint after Akien flagged flooding concern: relationship is attention-routing CONDITIONED BY the person, not attention ON the person. One singleton TWM observation at category='relationship_frame', salience 0.75 (below foreground), TTL 3600s, throttled 300s refresh. _resolve_relationship_frame maps _HUMAN_AUTHORS → PR_AKIEN (single-user for now). _push_relationship_frame uses twm_evict_category for singleton semantics; touches pr_facia last_activity_ts as side effect. _process_inner integration before user_input push. Non-human-author turns do not set a frame. Retrieval bias (cortex.search scoring hook) DEFERRED — needs its own sprint. goal_adopt pr_facia_id capture DEFERRED to T-pr-secondary-attractor-nesting. 9 new tests including the critical non-flood assertion. 1992 total pass, zero regressions.
body.description:
REFINED 2026-04-13 after Akien flagged flooding concern. A persistent relationship is a FRAME for attention-routing, not content-in-working-memory. When Leah walks in while Akien is coding, Leah doesn't flood working memory — she becomes the ambient context the coding is happening inside of. Attention stays on the code; Leah is felt through framing and routing bias, not content volume.

ARCHITECTURE — frame marker, not content flood:

1. At _process_inner entry, identify the interlocutor from author/thread. For now (single-user) any _HUMAN_AUTHORS turn on a human-facing channel maps to PR_AKIEN. Later tickets add per-thread resolution as Igor talks to more people.

2. Push ONE TWM observation at category='relationship_frame': 'FRAME|pr=PR_AKIEN|weight=1.00|display=Akien'. Salience ~0.75 — comfortably above background noise but BELOW foreground tasks so it does not compete with them. Singleton — call twm_evict_category('relationship_frame') first so only one frame is active. Longer TTL (~3600s+) so the frame persists across turns without being re-pushed every turn unless the interlocutor changes.

3. Retrieval bias, NOT pre-load. Add a hook in cortex.search() that applies a small salience bonus (~+0.1) to memories whose metadata links to the current frame's pr_facia_id. Relationship-colored retrieval without relationship-flooded working memory. Igor pulls Akien context when he reaches for something that matches; he is not crushed by it when he does not.

4. Foreground slots are for active topics, not for the relationship itself. The current task (goal_adopt), open reply-obligation, specific topic thread — those get pushed at 0.85-0.95 as foreground slots WITHIN the frame. Multiple can coexist. The frame is the backdrop; the slots are what Igor is attending to moment to moment. Matches the 'attention shifts across subtopics while Leah remains the primary one' intuition.

5. Nesting via metadata, not flooding. Goal_adopt during an active frame records pr_facia_id in the goal's metadata. Prompt builder can mention 'this is part of your work with Akien' as a single line, not by dumping 30 memories.

THE PRINCIPLE: a relationship is attention-routing conditioned by the person, not attention-on-the-person. The person is the frame attention happens inside of. Content volume is not how presence is felt — routing bias is. (Promote this to CLAUDE.md biomimicry section after shipping.)

DEPENDS ON: T-pr-schema-seed (shipped 53740afc).

DOES NOT: write to the facia subtree (T-pr-accretion), propagate weight (T-pr-investment-weight-propagation), consolidate during sleep (T-pr-consolidation), provide on-demand subtree scan tools (future pr_recent_threads etc).

TESTS:
- _process_inner on an Akien turn pushes exactly ONE relationship_frame observation at salience ~0.75
- Singleton behavior: second consecutive Akien turn does not stack — either re-push replaces via evict-category OR skip if frame already fresh
- Non-human-author turns (cron, consolidation, impulse) do NOT push a frame
- cortex.search() bias: a memory linked to PR_AKIEN subtree scores slightly higher than an identical non-linked memory when frame is active
- Background sources (heartbeats, reminders) at their usual salience still pass through — frame does not gate them out
- Goals adopted during an active frame carry pr_facia_id in metadata

INERTIA: MEDIUM. main.py _process_inner (MEDIUM) + cortex.search() scoring hook (MEDIUM). No models.py, no reasoners/base.py.
